reasoning: The military junta in Myanmar extended the state of emergency for another six months on January 31, 2025, which means it will remain in place until July 31, 2025 (after the resolution date of July 1, 2025). Furthermore, under the 2008 constitution, elections must be held within six months of the state of emergency being lifted, and the junta's election body has officially announced that polls are targeted for November 2025. Because the earliest official target date is five months after the deadline, and the state of emergency actively covers the entire period up to the deadline, it is virtually impossible that an election will be held before July 1, 2025. I assign a highly confident 2% probability.
